sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Data from non-randomized trials demonstrate improved durable response rates with PD-1/PD-L1 blockade compared with cytotoxic therapy. The safety profiles are significantly different, requiring clinician and patient education for managing immune-related adverse events [MCC-D, footnote b]. Clinical trial enrollment is preferred when available.

cards.name: Avelumab
cards.detail: Infusion-related reactions (common), fatigue, diarrhea, rash, nausea. Immune-related AEs occur in <20% of patients, typically grade 1-2 [MS-16].

cards.name: Pembrolizumab
cards.drugs: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks OR Pembrolizumab 400 mg IV every 6 weeks
cards.detail: Immune-related AEs (fatigue, rash, diarrhea, pruritus, hepatitis, colitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies). Treatment-related AEs in 68-77% of patients, grade 3/4 in 5-21% [MS-16].

cards.name: Retifanlimab-dlwr
cards.detail: Immune-related adverse events similar to other PD-1 inhibitors.

cards.name: Nivolumab
cards.detail: Immune-related adverse events, fatigue, rash, diarrhea, hepatitis, colitis, pneumonitis, endocrinopathies.

cards.name: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab
cards.drugs: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab (doses not specified in input)
cards.detail: Combination immunotherapy with higher immune-related toxicity, especially colitis and hepatitis.

cards.name: Cytotoxic Chemotherapy Regimens
cards.drugs: Carboplatin, Cisplatin, combinations with Etoposide, CAV, Topotecan
cards.detail: Standard cytotoxic chemotherapy toxicities: myelosuppression, nausea, alopecia, fatigue.

cards.name: Octreotide LAR
cards.detail: For somatostatin receptor positive tumors: well-tolerated, side effects include gallstones, bradycardia, hyperglycemia.

cards.name: Pazopanib
cards.detail: TKI toxicities: hypertension, liver enzyme elevation, fatigue, diarrhea, increased risk of bleeding.

cards.name: T-VEC
cards.detail: Local injection site reactions, flu-like symptoms, risk of herpetic infection.

cards.name: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab
cards.detail: Immune-related adverse events; used in surgical candidates [MCC-D 2 of 5].

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: Preferred Regimens: Primary Locally Advanced: Avelumab, Pembrolizumab. Recurrent Locally Advanced: Pembrolizumab, Retifanlimab-dlwr. Primary Regional: Avelumab, Nivolumab, Pembrolizumab. Recurrent Regional: Pembrolizumab, Retifanlimab-dlwr. Disseminated: Avelumab, Nivolumab, Pembrolizumab, Retifanlimab-dlwr. Adjuvant: None (clinical trial).
